---
title: "Piedmont Primrose-Willow (Ludwigia arcuata)"
slug: "ludwigia-arcuata"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621850244/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Piedmont Primrose-Willow"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Myrtales"
tax_family: "Onagraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Myrtales", "Onagraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621850244/medium.jpg" alt="Piedmont Primrose-Willow" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jimw3">
jimw3</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341575613">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Myrtales
-   **Family:** Onagraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=164849)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and wet flatwoods.

## Natural history notes

A slender wetland plant characterized by its narrow, linear leaves and small yellow flowers with four petals. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, pond margins, and wet flatwoods, often in sandy soils. It grows during the warmer months and commonly occurs in shallow water or seasonally flooded areas, contributing to aquatic and wetland plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/164849-Ludwigia-arcuata)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/ludwigia-arcuata/)
